original_abstract: Migrating computational intensive tasks from mobile devices to more resourceful cloud servers is a promising technique to increase the computational capacity of mobile devices while saving their battery energy. In this paper, we consider an MIMO multicell system where multiple mobile users (MUs) ask for computation offloading to a common cloud server. We formulate the offloading problem as the joint optimization of the radio resources-the transmit precoding matrices of the MUs-and the computational resources-the CPU cycles/second assigned by the cloud to each MU-in order to minimize the overall users' energy consumption, while meeting latency constraints. The resulting optimization problem is nonconvex (in the objective function and constraints). Nevertheless, in the single-user case, we are able to compute the global optimal solution in closed form. In the more challenging multiuser scenario, we propose an iterative algorithm, based on a novel successive convex approximation technique, converging to a local optimal solution of the original nonconvex problem. We then show that the proposed algorithmic framework naturally leads to a distributed and parallel implementation across the radio access points, requiring only a limited coordination/signaling with the cloud. Numerical results show that the proposed schemes outperform disjoint optimization algorithms.
humanized_abstract: The offloading of CPU intensive tasks from mobile devices to more capable resource points in the network, such as cloud servers, have emerged as efficient methods for increasing the processing capacity of mobile devices and thus prolonging their battery life times. In this paper, we investigate the benefits of computation offloading in an MIMO multicell network scenario, where multiple mobile users (MUs) share resources of their respective base stations (BSs) and remotely access the common cloud server to perform computation offloading. We formulate the radio and computation resource allocation for MUs, such that their energy consumption is minimized while satisfying their respective quality of experience (QoE) latency constraints. We express the problem as a joint optimization of precoding vectors (radio resource) and cloud computing resources. The formulated optimization problem is non-convex in terms of the objective function and constraints. For single user case, we obtain the global optimal solution in a closed form. In multiuser case, we propose a iterative algorithm using a novel Successive Convex Approximation (SCA) method that gives a local optimal solution of the non-convex objective function. In addition, we show that our proposed framework leads to a distributed and parallel implementation at each BS, while requiring minimal signaling with the cloud. Our simulations confirm that the proposed solutions significantly outperform disjoint optimization approaches.
